title: dryopteris setifera subsp. lobata
intro: Dryopteris setifera subsp. lobata, also known as the Lobed Wood Fern, is a subspecies of the Dryopteris setifera plant, a type of fern native to the mountainous regions of Asia. The Lobed Wood Fern has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of the Lobed Wood Fern typically involve drying and processing the fronds, which can then be used in teas, infusions, or as a topical application to reap its medicinal benefits.
